data.narrative:
Darkhive is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $22M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers—with particular focus on 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current valuation, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $44M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 comparable companies produces $225M, with a range spanning $125M to $500M. The disparity reflects material differences in growth trajectories and technology maturity within the Defense & Security segment at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $37M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $134M. This wide range reflects genuine uncertainty around Darkhive's relative positioning within a peer set exhibiting 4x variance. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployed; peer median is significantly higher, suggesting either that Darkhive is earlier in execution relative to comparables or that the peer set commands a sector premium. Resolution of this gap requires deeper diligence into product maturity, customer traction, and go-to-market execution relative to named peers.
